sessions.intent: Carve the singular-namespaces plan: rename app/** to singular+lowercase AND reshape app.X into collection-node accessors that kill the App* OBP wrappers, make app/context non-null, and promote type.@this to an entity owned by data.Type — all in one branch.

before.assumptions: Started as a folder/namespace rename (brief scope: PLang/app/** only, no logic). Design conversation with Ingi expanded it: the four App* wrapper aliases (AppGoals/AppChannels/AppEvents/AppModules) are OBP violations, so the rename must also reshape app.X into a collection node with [name]/.list/.current selectors; data owns its type; app and context are non-null invariants; type.@this should become a real entity behind data.Type. Ingi chose one branch for all four pieces.
before.risk: Large blast radius. Rename touches ~190 files (mostly alias-shielded). Accessor reshape touches ~286 registry call sites (Types 80, Variables 63, Channels 37 are the heavy ones). Source generator has STRING-LITERAL namespace refs and a tracking-name constant (prefix="app.modules.") the compiler won't catch — a miss there breaks generated-code compilation at consumer sites, not in the generator. Making app/context non-null will surface real un-stamped-Data reads as NREs (intended: they are the bugs the ?. hid). type.@this promotion reshapes the builder schema path.

actions.context: Pulled origin/singular-namespaces, read the brief, merged origin/runtime2 in (clean; touched channels/actor/this.cs only — no plan impact). Verified the brief's rename tables and the App* wrapper claims against the live tree. Found the generator does NOT auto-wire app nav properties (hand-written in app/this.cs), and the App* names are referenced in only 3-4 files each (alias-shielded).

Deep design walk with Ingi across type, channel, goal. Settled: app.X = collection node; .current only where execution has a current (callstack); registry=selection+lifecycle, behavior on element; data owns its type; app+context non-null. Resolved the 'where does the list live' knot: bare app.goal can't be the current without orphaning the collection — current rides as .current. _goals/_byPath are NOT duplicate (PrPath vs Path keys) — kept.

Wrote the spine + four deep-dive topic files. Inventory gathered via four parallel read-only scans (rename map, ~286 accessor call sites by subsystem, ~39 nullability sites + 3 nullable App back-refs + 9 nullable Context fields, generator strings + test mirrors + doc refs). Corrected two brief errors (PascalCase infra folders stay; serializers/channel-events collapse to singular).

Carved four stages in strict dependency order (1 rename → 2 non-null → 3 accessor → 4 type-entity), build green between each. Wrote test handoff: strategy (regression floor + 4 integration cuts) + coverage matrix/failure matrix/new-surfaces. Stage 4 (type-entity, builder schema reshape) flagged as the integration risk and natural cut point.

after.health: green — plan carved, no code changed
after.notes: Plan is design-complete and ready for test-designer then coder. Open item for coder+test-designer: the index-miss policy setting (name + default). Stage 4 is the integration risk (builder LLM schema must be golden-pinned). The generator string literals are the highest-risk mechanical item in stage 1 — verify by clean rebuild. CLAUDE.md OBP-block token updates filed for the docs bot (do not edit CLAUDE.md directly).

sessions.intent: Address Ingi's review comments on the plan, then recreate the stage files and test-handoff files with the review changes baked in.

before.assumptions: Ingi reviewed the five plan files via the review server and left comments; he then deleted the stage/test files for a focused design review and, once comments were addressed, asked to recreate them.
before.risk: Stage/test files must stay consistent with the revised design (module not demoted; ctx→context + extra nullable back-refs; type=PLang System.Type with data.Type.ClrType). Any drift between the deep dives and the stage files would mislead the coder.

actions.context: Addressed 5 review comments (all agreed): module keeps this.cs as a no-.current service at app.module (reversed my demote); ctx→context everywhere + swept for other over-nullable back-refs (5 structural flip, 2 init-only held); type.@this = PLang's System.Type; data.Type.ClrType (CLR type lives on the entity). Replied + resolved each via the review-server API.
Recreated the four stage files and the two test-handoff files with the review changes baked in. Restored stage/test links in plan.md and the stage-status table in summary.md.

after.health: green — plan + stages + tests consistent, no code changed
after.notes: Plan is complete and ready for test-designer. All review comments resolved. Stage files and test handoff reflect the revised design (module not demoted, ctx→context, type entity = PLang System.Type with data.Type.ClrType).
